headline: What if every vulnerability had a price tag?
One search. Every exploit. Instantly.
body: Sploitas is a search engine built specifically for security researchers. Type a software version and get every known exploit, CVE, and vulnerability mapped to it. No more digging through 15 different databases.

headline: 3 seconds instead of 3 hours
body: The average time to patch a critical vulnerability is 197 days. During that window, attackers are searching for the exact same exploits. Sploitas makes that search take 3 seconds instead of 3 hours.

headline: 40% of security time wasted on recon
body: Enterprise security teams spend 40% of their time on reconnaissance: finding what is vulnerable, checking exploit availability, assessing real-world risk. A dedicated search engine cuts that time by 8-10x.

headline: 23,000+ new CVEs per year. Tracked in real time.
body: There are 23,000+ new CVEs published every year. No human can track them all. Sploitas indexes and cross-references them in real time, connecting exploits to affected systems automatically.

headline: Defenders finally match attacker speed
body: The offensive security community has been fragmented across dozens of forums, databases, and paste sites. A unified search engine changes the power dynamic. Defenders can now match attacker speed in vulnerability research.

headline: Follow for more on cybersecurity tools
